svg.text: TTLSim 20V8 burns — GAL20V8 test harness on Arduino Mega 2560
Single-row wiring, same Mega pin roles as the 16V8 harness. Unused GAL inputs strap to GND at the socket. No series resistors needed.
chip-pin labels read: simple / registered / complex

g.text: C1: 100 nF ceramic across pins 24–12, close to the socket

svg.text: No direction-changing lines in this harness — D22 / D24 and their series resistors are not used at all.
Physical view — bottom-edge header, left → right (outer row only)
board edge below • the GND wire also feeds the socket-side strap rail for pins 7–11, 14 and 23
Per-burn pin assignments (pin 24 = 5 V, pin 12 = GND on all three)

g.text: ¹ Unused GAL inputs (7–11, 14, 23) are strapped to GND directly at the socket — no Mega pins needed, keeping the wiring single-row. CMOS inputs must not float.
² Hi-Z check for y2: INPUT_PULLUP on D27, apply a = b (driven y2 would be 0) with ena = 0 — reading 1 proves the pin floated. ³ /OE is GAL pin 13 here (still D23);

the registered /OE and clocking procedures are identical to the 16V8. ⁴ Pins 15/18/19 stay unconnected: unprogrammed OLMCs — 18/19 are the mode-2-forcing pins and may drive; never wire them to a Mega output.
